edges.description: Outer retinal dysfunction maps to the patient's localized visual field loss.
RPE atrophy and ellipsoid layer loss can leave persistent scotoma.
edges.source: Retinal pigment epithelium disruption and atrophy

edges.description: Immune-mediated injury is a proposed upstream driver of outer retinal disruption.

edges.source: Antiviral therapy for suspected viral or herpetic trigger

nodes.description: Acute annular outer retinopathy primarily affects the outer retina, producing a gray annular border at the edge of acute visual field loss and subsequent outer retinal degeneration.

evidence.reference_title: Acute annular outer retinopathy as a variant of acute zonal occult outer retinopathy.
evidence.snippet: The cause of this disorder, which affects primarily the outer retina, was not determined.

nodes.description: Antiviral therapy, such as valacyclovir or intravenous acyclovir, has been attempted in anecdotal AAOR reports when a viral or herpetic trigger was considered, but no established antiviral efficacy has been demonstrated.
nodes.id: Antiviral therapy for suspected viral or herpetic trigger

evidence.reference_title: Acute annular outer retinopathy: report of four cases.
evidence.snippet: As more cases are recognized, the characteristic features of the disease spectrum, the etiology, and treatment options may be better elucidated.

nodes.description: No established disease-modifying therapy exists; reported cases include observation, corticosteroids, and antivirals with variable outcomes, so monitoring is used to document progression or stabilization.

evidence.reference_title: Acute annular outer retinopathy: report of four cases.
evidence.snippet: As more cases are recognized, the characteristic features of the disease spectrum, the etiology, and treatment options may be better elucidated.

nodes.description: Structural retinal pigment epithelium disruption and atrophy occur within the annular lesion and correlate with fundus autofluorescence, OCT, and visual field abnormalities.
nodes.id: Retinal pigment epithelium disruption and atrophy

evidence.reference_title: Acute annular outer retinopathy preceded by invasive ductal breast carcinoma: a case report.
evidence.snippet: We identified RPE atrophy with nodular hyperreflectivity and loss of ellipsoid layer within the white annular ring with corresponding visual field loss.

nodes.description: Acute localized visual field loss is a defining presentation and can expand briefly before stabilizing.

evidence.reference_title: Acute annular outer retinopathy as a variant of acute zonal occult outer retinopathy.
evidence.snippet: A 23-year-old man developed a rapid-onset, large, dense scotoma that was associated with a peculiar gray intraretinal ring corresponding to the edge of the scotoma and normal fluorescein angiographic findings.

nodes.description: The initiating cause is unknown, but reports support an immune-mediated model based on an immune-ring hypothesis and occasional systemic autoimmune or malignancy associations.

evidence.reference_title: Acute annular outer retinopathy as a variant of acute zonal occult outer retinopathy.
evidence.snippet: We speculate that this disorder is part of the spectrum of acute zonal occult outer retinopathy and that the gray border, which separates the normal from the abnormal retina, represents an immune ring phenomenon.

nodes.description: Systemic corticosteroids have been attempted in individual AAOR cases when an inflammatory or immune-mediated process is suspected, but evidence is anecdotal and outcomes are mixed.

evidence.reference_title: Acute annular outer retinopathy: report of four cases.
evidence.snippet: As more cases are recognized, the characteristic features of the disease spectrum, the etiology, and treatment options may be better elucidated.

nodes.description: Visual field defects correspond to the annular outer retinal lesion and may be persistent.

evidence.reference_title: Acute annular outer retinopathy: report of four cases.
evidence.snippet: Four healthy patients developed the acute onset of visual field loss associated with a localized, white annular outer retinopathy.
